summary: Bryan Cantrill announces he will be giving a talk and demo on DTrace at the BayLISA meeting in Cupertino. He emphasizes that he'll be demonstrating DTrace running on OpenSolaris bits that he downloaded and built himself from outside Sun's internal network, highlighting the newly open nature of the project.
key_points: DTrace demo at BayLISA meeting in Cupertino
Running OpenSolaris built from publicly available source code
Demonstrates accessibility of OpenSolaris to external developers
money_quotes: I will be demo'ing DTrace tonight on my laptop running OpenSolaris bits that I downloaded and built myself from outside of Sun's internal network.
key_insight: The ability to build and run OpenSolaris entirely from publicly available sources outside Sun's network marked a significant milestone in the project's openness.
